body:
The bed reeked of cinnamon, sex, and sweat, as I awoke tangled in the sheets from where she left before. I know it wasn’t possible, but I swear I could still feel the warmth of her body on them, as if the sheets themselves were letting me continue to be close to her, despite her absence.

I looked around at the empty delivery containers that littered the bedroom, how many days had we spent had we spent lost in each other, before the world reminded us there was more to it than my bedroom? I rolled onto my tummy, burying my face into her pillow, losing myself in her scent and the memory of the past few days.

I still feel phantom fingers tracing whatever popped into her head on my skin, the way she’d pause and then giggle to watch my goosebumps arise, only to trace over them again, as if they had some secret in braille just for her. I pondered a shower, but struggled to let myself move from my imaginings of her warmth, surrounded by her scent, still lost in the last few days that were wholly ours.

Every time I ponder getting up, I hesitate, not wanting the amazing past few days to end. I know I’m being dumb, but I don’t want to disturb that
reblog_comment:
The bed reeked of cinnamon, sex, and sweat, as I awoke tangled in the sheets from where she left before. I know it wasn’t possible, but I swear I could still feel the warmth of her body on them, as if the sheets themselves were letting me continue to be close to her, despite her absence.

I looked around at the empty delivery containers that littered the bedroom, how many days had we spent had we spent lost in each other, before the world reminded us there was more to it than my bedroom? I rolled onto my tummy, burying my face into her pillow, losing myself in her scent and the memory of the past few days.

I still feel phantom fingers tracing whatever popped into her head on my skin, the way she’d pause and then giggle to watch my goosebumps arise, only to trace over them again, as if they had some secret in braille just for her. I pondered a shower, but struggled to let myself move from my imaginings of her warmth, surrounded by her scent, still lost in the last few days that were wholly ours.

Every time I ponder getting up, I hesitate, not wanting the amazing past few days to end. I know I’m being dumb, but I don’t want to disturb that magic that we experienced together, fearing on some level, if I move too much, it will end up all being a dream, since I dared to break the bedspell…

I’m under your bedspell
I can’t fight but I don’t wanna
And I won’t tell anybody how I fall
When I get off tonight I know that I’ll be under you in this bedspell
You can break the bed just don’t break the bedspell
